# Transcrição: 2_things_that_form_your_FIRST_impression

- **Fonte:** 2_things_that_form_your_FIRST_impression
- **Duração:** 01:04
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:04]

Since you've all met me online, all of you have formed assumptions and beliefs about who I am as a person. Whether I'm successful, whether I'm trustworthy, whether I'm friendly, whether I'm caring, whether I'm kind. All of these things. You've formed them all already, whether they're true or not. In our society, we tend to focus too much time on our visual image. And our visual image is the way we dress in combination with the way we use our body language. And based on your visual image, people will form assumptions about who you are. We'll form lots of assumptions. Positive or negative. Whether they're true or not. If I stand here with good posture, you will make the assumption, oh, that person seems rather confident. If I wear a smile on my face, you'll make the assumption, oh, that person seems really friendly. And then when you speak, you reveal your vocal image. That's the image that other people create in their minds about who you are the moment you speak. And when they've got these two pieces of information, visual and vocal, they will now form beliefs about who you are as a person, whether they're true or not.
